footer.tagline: The first support platform for teenage girls in sport
footer.ctaText: Join the waitlist and help us Break All Barriers for the Next-Gen Athletes

home.badge: Breaking All Barriers for the Next Gen Athletes
home.heroTitle: One in two girls drops out of sport at puberty because she feels wrong.
home.heroBody: BAB helps adolescent athletes recognise, understand and manage their body's signals precisely when it becomes most unpredictable, so they keep playing and having fun — performing at their best and feeling their best.
home.coachDesc: Help your athletes understand their body's signals and stay in sport, with secure, aggregated data.
home.parentDesc: Support your daughter through puberty changes, ensuring total privacy and healthy sports.
home.bivioSub: BAB enables the athletes’ support system to accompany them through their growth and development on and off the pitch.
home.bivioCoachDesc: BAB facilitates and improves the communication with your athletes, so you have all you need to support them through puberty and adolescence.
home.bivioCoachP2: Help them get stronger, more confident and comfortable with their bodies
home.bivioParentDesc: BAB provides practical tools and interactive resources to help you support your daughter’s development, wellbeing, and performance — through an approach tailored to her individual needs.
home.bivioParentP1: Insights that respect her privacy and personal space
home.dataTitle:
Women are treated as small men,
and girls as small women.
home.data1Desc: Believe their menstrual cycle negatively affects athletic performance
home.data2Desc: Of all sports research is conducted exclusively on women athletes
home.data3Desc: Scientific research on injuries in adolescent female athletes
home.testimonialsTitle: What Our Community Says About What BAB Is Building
home.missionTitle: BAB is your coach off the pitch.
home.missionBody: With quizzes, games and sporting metaphors, it supports girls’ growth in sport — teaching them to track, listen to, manage and communicate how they feel to teammates, coaches and parents.
home.missionBodyHighlight: So they face puberty without feeling wrong or alone on their journey.
home.silenceLead: The same voice, before and after BAB.
silenceOldVoices: I keep it all to myself
I'm scared I'm not good anymore
silenceBabVoices: I know and value my body
I work with my body, not against it
I don't have to do it alone
I play better because I know myself better
I can explain what I need
home.marquee: 🕵️‍♀️ GDPR Compliant ✦ 🧪 Backed by Science ✦ 👟 Built with Elite Athletes ✦ ✨ Puberty Support ✦
home.footerTitle:
Reach Your Personal Best,
By Feeling Your Best.
home.privacy1: Only if you give consent, the data you track contributes to anonymous scientific research aimed at improving the health, wellbeing, and performance of both you and other young athletes. Together, we're helping close the gaps in youth and sports research.
home.privacy2: Your data is always anonymous, protected, and used solely to advance teenagers' health, wellbeing, development, and performance.
home.heroPhotoAlt: Teenage athlete during training, black-and-white portrait
home.dataPhotoAlt: Portrait of a BAB athlete next to data on female sport drop-out

(2021), "Invisible Sportswomen", WSPAJ 29(2): 6% of studies on women only. ③ Horan et al. (2024), Sports Medicine: only 32 studies on injuries in under-19 athletes. Full references on request.
home.marqueeElite: Built with Clinical Experts and Elite Athletes

coach.accessibleNote: Aggregated team data · athletes' privacy always protected
coach.headline: Help your athletes stay in sport — and your club keep them.
coach.sub: One in two girls drops out of sport during puberty. BAB gives your athletes the tools to listen to their bodies and stay on the pitch. You only receive aggregated, anonymous team signals — never the cycle or individual health.
coach.value1Desc: See when the team is struggling and step in before girls quit: fewer drop-outs, a stronger squad.
coach.value2Desc: Aggregated fatigue signals help you balance load and recovery at the right time, before overload becomes a problem.
coach.value3Desc: Only aggregated, anonymous data, compliant with GDPR for minors. The cycle and individual health are never visible: families trust you, and you’re protected.
coach.pricingValue: Annual fee per club, based on the number of teams. Free for athletes.
coach.privacyInline: You see only aggregated, anonymous data. Never individual biological data.
coach.signal1Desc: Above the group average this week. Consider a lighter session.
coach.signal1Cta: Send a note to the team
coach.signal2Desc: They asked how to manage energy and training on tougher days.
coach.how1Desc: From the free app they log energy, mood and body signals, privately.
coach.how2Desc: Only aggregated, anonymous data: participation, average energy and group flags.
coach.how3Desc: Adjust load, send a note or answer the team’s questions — at the right time.
coach.ctaReassure: We're launching with our first partner clubs. Free, no-commitment demo — we'll get back to you within a few days.

about.missionLead: Redefining what it means for teenage girls to play sports when their bodies feel unpredictable — supporting them to tune in to and leverage their bodies’ signals instead of ignoring them. <mark style="background-color:#D2EC7C;color:#0F0F12;box-decoration-break:clone;-webkit-box-decoration-break:clone;padding:0 0.15em;">Because no one should have to choose between playing sports and feeling good.</mark>
about.story1: At thirteen, Mia's body no longer felt like home. She had grown taller in a matter of months, and before she understood what was happening, hormones had changed more than just her body. They changed how she played and how she felt while doing it.
about.story2pre: Pushing through period symptoms, fatigue and hunger, she learned that
about.story2post: . Disconnecting from her body became her winning strategy.
about.story3: Soon, injuries and anxiety started creeping in and her performance began to drop. And because no one talked about any of it, Mia kept quiet and started questioning whether she was no longer made for sport.
about.story4pre: But it wasn't Mia who had fallen behind.
about.story4highlight: It was sport that failed her
about.story4post: by neglecting her changing body, health and wellbeing.
about.bigCaption: Just like Mia, several athletes pay the cost of bleeding in sport.

stats.label: Report a negative period-related experience in sport
Feel pressured to push through period pain
Ignore period pain as their main coping mechanism
Believe missing periods during competition is normal

about.founderBadge2: Former Marketing Specialist @ adidas (~5 yrs) & PUMA (2.5 yrs)
about.founderQuote1: BAB was born from the personal experience of its founder, Gaia Manzone. After spending a decade working for adidas and PUMA, Gaia witnessed firsthand the systemic barriers faced by elite female athletes. But her connection to the issue started much earlier, when health challenges arising from managing puberty and performance without the adequate support forced her to stop competing.
about.founderQuote2: Gaia believes that without women’s health, there can be no women’s sport. That belief led her to bring together a trusted network of elite athletes, doctors, researchers, and sport experts to create BAB: a platform built for adolescents, their parents, their coaches, and the scientists working tirelessly to drive change, but often without the resources, tools, or support they need to do so.
about.founderQuote3: BAB exists to ensure that no young athlete has to navigate puberty, hormones, health, and performance alone.
about.photoAlt: Young athlete from the BAB community
about.statsSources: Sources: “State of the Period 2025”, The Harris Poll for PERIOD & Thinx (1,014 U.S. teens, ages 13–19) — 93% negative experiences in sport from menstrual symptoms, 73% pressure to “tough it out” without complaining about pain. British Journal of Sports Medicine 2024;58(Suppl 2):A203 — 84% and 36%. Full references on request.

simulator.footer: 30 seconds a day. Like a Tamagotchi — but it's you. 🌱
simulator.buddyDone: THANK YOU! You kept me at my best today 💛
simulator.buddyLow: Hey… I’m a bit down today. Can you help me?
simulator.buddyOk: I feel better! Keep it up 💪

hold.title: Pour the water
hold.hint: Hold until the glass is full

cuddle.title: Cuddle the Buddy
cuddle.hint: Tap to show it your affection

breathe.title: Breathe with it
breathe.hint: A slow breath together: 4 seconds in, 4 out

done.day: Day 1 · come back tomorrow for the streak
done.privacy: You took 30 seconds for yourself. Coaches and parents only see a signal — never your cycle, mood or details.
done.coachMsg: Mia is energised today. Great moment to push on technique.
done.parentMsg: Mia is doing well. Cheer for her at training too, not just on race day.
done.ctaQuestion: Want BAB for your daughter or your team?

checkin.intro: Hi! How do you feel today? 30 seconds for you.

q1.prompt: Your "inner team": who plays when you're tired?
q1.explain: Your body is a team: when one part runs low, the others cover. Listening to it makes you stronger, not weaker.

q2.prompt: Body traffic light: what do you do on "amber"?
q2.opt3: Adjust the load and listen to the signals
q2.explain: Amber isn't a stop: it's "go with care". Adjusting the load keeps you in the game longer.

reveal.title: The same day, 3 points of view
reveal.intro: You decide what to share. See who sees what.
reveal.athleteBody: Cycle, energy, mood and today's progress — in full, just for you.
reveal.coachNotSees: Does NOT see: your cycle, your mood, your name.
reveal.parentBody: A gentle summary: "she's doing well, cheer her on at training too".
reveal.parentNotSees: Does NOT see: no cycle data, no clinical detail.

items.a:
BAB is your coach off the pitch; designed with clinical experts to support teenage girls and non-binary athletes to navigate, understand and leverage the impact puberty, ovarian hormones and adolescence have on their performance, health and wellbeing.
We believe that teens should not have to figure it all out alone. That's why BAB also gives parents, caregivers, coaches, and clubs access to the right tools and resources to better understand and support young athletes throughout their journey.

BAB is primarily designed to support girls and young people who experience periods and practice sport.
The app also offers personalised insights and educational resources developed by our research and medical teams, as well as a private and anonymous Q&A feature where teens and parents can ask questions and receive answers from elite athletes and healthcare professionals.

items.q: What features does the BAB app include?
items.a:
BAB utilises gamification to help teenagers recognise, understand, communicate and manage the signals their bodies are sending them. It also includes period tracking, energy tracking, and mood tracking, helping teenage athletes understand how these factors influence both their athletic performance and everyday life.
The platform also offers personalised insights and educational resources developed by our research and medical teams, as well as a private and anonymous Q&A feature where teens and parents can ask questions and receive answers from elite athletes and healthcare professionals.

items.q: Is BAB safe, anonymous, and private?
items.a:
Yes. BAB is fully compliant with EU GDPR regulations, and we are committed to keeping your data safe, secure, private, and anonymous.
We also ensure that all content and features are reviewed by medical professionals and sports experts to guarantee that they are age-appropriate, evidence-based, and safe for young athletes.

BAB was founded by Gaia Manzone in collaboration with a multidisciplinary team of sports researchers, doctors, elite athletes, and coaches who are passionate about improving health, wellbeing, and performance outcomes for young athletes.

lisa.quote: BAB made me feel seen and realise how much I could have benefitted if something like this had existed during my upbringing in sport. It took me many years to understand that strength is not the opposite of femininity, and the next generation of female athletes should never have to choose between becoming powerful and fitting in.

alisha.quote: Young girls face misinformation and scientific bias, challenges that are often amplified in sports settings, particularly during the profound physical and psychological changes of puberty. BAB provides a safe, preventive solution while contributing to the advancement of research in a field that has been overlooked for far too long.

vasundhara.quote: My daughter is 11 and competes at regional and national levels in fencing. A tool like BAB helps me understand how to best support her athletic goals in a safe and informed way, particularly as she navigates the changes of adolescence while continuing to pursue high-level competition.

erica.quote: As a professional volleyball player and coach, I never had such a great resource as BAB to support the specific needs my teenage athletes and I have.

m1.title: TRACK
m1.desc: Monitor energy levels, sleep and mood, underpinned by menstrual health and growth metrics to build a complete picture of the athlete's wellbeing through longitudinal inputs.

m2.title: UNDERSTAND
m2.desc: Turn the data into clear, simple and engaging language, with explanations of your own data and body-education resources, session after session.

m3.title: OPTIMISE
m3.desc: Puts physical and emotional wellbeing first, and builds performance from there: personalised daily recommendations, defined by clinical experts.

m4.title: PREVENT
m4.desc: Helps you recognise your body’s signals early, with educational reminders and prompts, before they can affect health, wellbeing or performance.

m5.title: SUPPORT
m5.desc: Create a stronger support system around the athlete both on and off the pitch with dedicated tools for parents and coaches.

faqs.q1: How does biological data privacy work?
faqs.a1: Sensitive data, including the menstrual cycle, is encrypted and protected, and individual signals are never shared: the coach only sees anonymous aggregates or generic alerts (e.g. 'Deload recommended').
faqs.q2: Is it suitable for all sports?
faqs.a2: Yes, BAB adapts to the workloads of every sport, whether team or individual.
faqs.q3: Is the app free for athletes?
faqs.a3: Yes, the mobile app for athletes is completely free. Sports clubs subscribe for access to the Coach Dashboard.

legal.contact: For any question about how we handle your data or to exercise your rights, write to us at info@babsport.com.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: BAB (Breaking All Barriers) cares about the privacy of athletes and those who support them. This policy explains what data we collect through this site, why, with whom we share it and what rights you have.

sections.p: The controller of the data collected through this site is Gaia Manzone, as the person responsible for the BAB — Breaking All Barriers project. You can contact the controller at any time at info@babsport.com.

We only collect the data you provide voluntarily through the site forms: your email address and, optionally, the sport you practise, your area of interest, your role, the name of the club and the message in a demo request. We do not collect individual health data through this site. Providing data is optional, but without a valid email address we cannot get back to you.

We process your data on the basis of your consent (Art. 6.1.a GDPR) to contact you about the waitlist, demos and BAB updates. Any analytics on site usage, if enabled, are aggregated and anonymous on the basis of our legitimate interest (Art. 6.1.f GDPR) in improving the service, and only after your consent to analytics cookies.

The forms on this site are intended for adults (coaches, club managers, parents). We do not knowingly collect data from minors through this site. Where processing concerns underage athletes, it takes place exclusively with the consent of those holding parental responsibility. If you believe a minor has provided us data without such consent, write to us and we will delete it promptly.

To run the service we rely on providers that process data on our behalf as processors: leads are stored via Supabase, on infrastructure located in the European Union (Dublin region, Ireland). If we later enable analytics tools (Google Analytics 4, Meta Pixel) we will update this page and load them only with your consent. We do not sell or share your data with third parties for marketing purposes.

sections.h: 6. Where we store data and transfers
sections.p: Data collected through the forms is stored on servers located in the European Union and is not transferred outside the EU. Should we in future enable tools involving a transfer to third countries (e.g. US analytics), such transfer will take place only on the basis of appropriate safeguards under the GDPR, such as the Standard Contractual Clauses.

We apply appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect data: encrypted transmission (HTTPS), access limited to the controller and database-level security rules that prevent public reading of the collected data.
We keep data for as long as necessary for validation and contact purposes, and in any case until you withdraw your consent. After that it is deleted or anonymised.

You have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict or object to the processing of your data and to data portability. To exercise these rights or withdraw consent at any time, write to info@babsport.com. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante, www.garanteprivacy.it).
We may update this policy over time. The version in force is always the one published on this page, with the update date shown at the top. We invite you to review it periodically.

cookie.title: Cookie Policy
cookie.intro: This page explains how BAB uses cookies and similar technologies on this site.

sections.p: Cookies are small text files a site saves on your device. They make the site work and, in some cases, measure its usage. Similar technologies, such as local storage (localStorage), serve comparable functions.

We use technical local storage to remember your language preference and your cookie choice. They are necessary for the site to work, do not profile users and, under the applicable rules, do not require consent.

sections.h: 3. Analytics and marketing cookies (only with consent)
sections.p: At present the site does not install third-party analytics or marketing cookies. If we enable them in future (e.g. Google Analytics 4 and Meta Pixel) to understand how the site is used, they will be loaded only after your explicit consent via the banner, and this page will be updated with the specific tools and durations.

You can accept or reject non-essential cookies from the banner shown on your first visit. You can change your mind at any time by clearing the site cookies and data from your browser: the banner will reappear and you can make a new choice.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.intro: By using this site you accept the following terms. Please read them carefully.

sections.p: This site presents BAB — Breaking All Barriers and collects expressions of interest (waitlist and demo requests). It is informational and promotional in nature and does not constitute a binding commercial offer.

Joining the waitlist or sending a demo request does not constitute a contract and does not guarantee access to the product. We will get back to you with details when the service is available. You agree to provide truthful information and not to submit unlawful, offensive or third-party content without the right to do so.

The content of this site and of the BAB project is for informational and educational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice, diagnosis or treatment. It is in no way a substitute for consulting a qualified doctor or healthcare professional. No information here creates a doctor-patient relationship. For any health-related decision, always consult a qualified professional; BAB is not responsible for choices made on the basis of the site content.

The name "BAB", "Breaking All Barriers", the logo, graphics, texts, code and any other site content are owned by the project holder and protected by copyright and trademark law. They may not be copied, reproduced, distributed or used, in whole or in part, without written authorisation.

The site is provided "as is" and "as available", without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee that the site will always be available, error-free or uninterrupted, and we reserve the right to change, suspend or discontinue its content or features at any time, even without notice.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, the holder is not liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from the use of or inability to use the site, nor for any misuse of the information provided. Mandatory consumer rights granted by law remain unaffected.

The site may contain links to third-party sites or services. We do not control and are not responsible for their content, policies or practices: visiting such sites is at your own risk and subject to their terms.
We may update these terms over time. The version in force is always the one published on this page, with the date shown at the top. Continued use of the site after any changes constitutes acceptance.
If any provision of these terms is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect.

These terms are governed by Italian law. Any dispute falls under the courts of the place of residence or domicile of the project holder; provided that, if you are a consumer, the mandatory jurisdiction of your place of residence or domicile under the law applies.
